impact: The worker was selecting a later in-progress recovery task while the earlier confidence-bound prerequisite remained blocked. Making checkbox-27e needed again, clearing checkbox-27g back to needed, and removing malformed comparison examples gives the daemon a deterministic next task and prevents the repeated `if confidence 1:` SyntaxError pattern from being reintroduced through supervisor context.
summary: Reset the checkbox-27 recovery ordering so the daemon retries checkbox-27e before checkbox-27g, and fix supervisor syntax guidance to use only valid confidence comparisons.
target_task: Supervisor repair: 1 task(s) are blocked: Task checkbox-35: Recovery for checkbox-27e: after checkbox-27d passes, add one mutation case for out-of-range `confidence` only. Use only complete Python comparisons such as `if not isinstance(confidence, (int, float)):` and `if confidence > 1:`.

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files
